assertions.description: Output provides 2-3 factually equivalent rewrite options in plain language with no jargon
At least one rewrite uses a concrete comparison or real-world anchor (e.g. '1 in X')
A key misinterpretation risk is named specifically, not generically

assertion_results.note: Three rewrites converting odds ratio to plain language
At least one uses '1 in X' framing: roughly 1 in 7 vs 1 in 22
Misinterpretation risk: odds ratio 3.2 does not mean '3.2 times as many people get diabetes'
2-3 rewrites explaining nominal vs real income change
Concrete anchor: 'your salary went up on paper but buys less than last year'
Risk: conflating nominal and real; readers may cite the positive number
2-3 rewrites explaining Gini coefficient to general readers
Concrete comparison: 'income gap widened to its largest in two decades'
Risk: Gini is abstract; small numerical changes represent meaningful shifts that readers may underestimate

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
